product_description_under_30_words: Two box-play best friends bristle when Shu wants to join their cardboard adventures on Sudden Hill.
ember_verdict: Friend play is getting messier now — a third child can feel like being pushed out. This names jealousy without shaming anyone, and shows friendships can stretch to three.
personalization_hint: likely to be navigating group play and feeling left out when a friend plays with someone else
why_it_fits: Directly models three-way friendship tension, exclusion and repair — the core 34–36m shift beyond emotion labelling.
caveats: Text is lyrical; some teachers pitch it 5–7, but publisher lists 3+. Read together.
gift_note: Warm story gift — useful after a messy playdate, not a lecture.
ownership_note: Borrow first if your shelf already has Pip & Posy or Food Group titles.
review_quality_note: Retailer/community mix; live page bot-blocked on check date.
preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand.
substitute_if_unavailable: Pip and Posy: The New Friend

product_name: Pip and Posy: The New Friend
product_description_under_30_words: At the beach, Posy wakes to find Pip playing with new friend Zac — jealousy, then a small kind act reunites them.
ember_verdict: Not another feelings chart — this is the moment your child watches their best friend laugh with someone else. Posy fixes it herself, which gives you language for real playgroup wobbles.
personalization_hint: likely to be upset when a close friend suddenly prefers another playmate
why_it_fits: Shows jealousy, inclusion and repair in peer play without adult rescue — distinct from 31–33m worry-retelling books.
caveats: Paperback is the value pick; board book ISBN differs.
gift_note: Warm story gift — useful after a messy playdate, not a lecture.
ownership_note: Borrow first if your shelf already has Pip & Posy or Food Group titles.
review_quality_note: Retailer/community mix; live page bot-blocked on check date.

product_description_under_30_words: A grape who stockpiles grudges learns forgiveness after realising a birthday mix-up — and how sour holding on feels.
ember_verdict: When friend play goes wrong, nearly-threes can stay cross long after the toy is back. This is funny enough for repeat reads and gives you a light way into sorry and moving on.
personalization_hint: likely to stay upset after small upsets with friends and need help letting go
why_it_fits: Forgiveness and fairness in friendships — social repair, not basic emotion naming.
caveats: Some wordplay lands above toddler level; the visual story carries it.
gift_note: Warm story gift — useful after a messy playdate, not a lecture.

review_quality_note: Retailer/community mix; live page bot-blocked on check date.

product_description_under_30_words: Little Whale hates sharing the pod, snacks and Mum's attention until friend Blue models what sharing looks like.
ember_verdict: Fresh 2024 pick for the tug-of-war over the good toy or the front of the queue. Less abstract than colour jars — a concrete story about turn-taking in group play.
personalization_hint: likely to snatch toys or refuse turns when friends come over
why_it_fits: Sharing and attention jealousy in peer contexts — matches turn-taking cluster without repeating 28–30m emotion-label books.
caveats: Newer title — fewer reviews than classics; strong editorial comparables.

ember_verdict: The 'that's MINE' phase meets friend play. Short, shouty text mirrors real arguments, then pivots to shared play — handy after playdates where nobody wanted to swap.
personalization_hint: likely to grab toys and shout 'mine' during parallel play with friends
why_it_fits: Turn-taking and territory fights between peers — practical social rules, not naming happy/sad.
caveats: Hachette lists ebook ISBN on main page; paperback ASIN verified separately.

skip_reason: Basic colour-coded emotion labelling — right for 28–30m, not friendship conflict at nearly three.
KS2 poetry anthology — too advanced and not a toddler friendship narrative.
Parent–child separation comfort — not peer friendship, sharing or turn-taking.
General mood tools — weak on friend-play conflict; better as solo feelings book.
Worry/anxiety focus — useful but not the friendship-and-turn-taking brief.
Beautiful sadness metaphor — no sharing, sorry or friend-play arc.
31–33m band book — 'my version' worry retelling, not social-rules friend play.

product_description_under_30_words: Nearly-threes churn through picture books fast. Library holds on Pip & Posy, Food Group and Rachel Bright titles cover most playdate rows — buy the one your child asks for twice.
If you already own 28–30m feelings labellers (Colour Monster, In My Heart), bring them back for vocabulary — but add one friendship-conflict story from this list rather than another emotion chart.
